Ad Text:
Careers with Impact

More than one million Australians trust us with their money, and that trust shapes how we work every day. Our members sit at the centre of everything we do, guiding our decisions and the impact we strive to make.

We’re looking for people who are purpose-driven and accountable, who value inclusion and collaboration, and who are proactive in managing risk to protect our members’ long-term outcomes. In return, you’ll be part of an organisation where your work contributes to strong returns, real-world impact, and a culture that reflects the communities our members serve.


Bring your authentic and passionate self to this exceptional role #careerswithimpact


Join HESTA's internal Fixed Income team and play a pivotal role in shaping the credit strategy of one of Australia's leading superannuation funds. This is an opportunity to do meaningful work — managing portfolios that deliver real retirement outcomes for HESTA members.


•    Deliver fundamental credit research across corporates, banks, and structured products — initiating coverage, conducting periodic reviews, and driving trade recommendations.
•    Maintain internal credit scores and shadow ratings, underpinned by detailed financial models and sector analysis.
•    Contribute to portfolio management strategy across internally managed Fixed Income, Enhanced Cash, and Domestic Private Debt portfolios.
•    Support the team's right of veto on domestic private credit transactions through rigorous due diligence.
•    Help implement and develop HESTA's ESG and Responsible Investment policies within the fixed income portfolio.
•    Build and manage external market relationships to source new insights and stay across credit market thematics.

This role could be based in Sydney or Melbourne.

About You

•    Demonstrated credit analyst experience of Australian corporate credit at a super fund, fund manager, investment bank or research house
•    Strong financial modelling and credit risk analytics skills
•    Experience analysing debt security issuers and conducting relative value assessment
•    Track record delivering to deadlines across multiple stakeholders
•    Rigorous analytical thinker with sharp attention to detail
•    Motivated by outcomes and continuous improvement
•    Strong communicator who can present complex credit views clearly
•    Comfortable in a collaborative, values-driven team environment
•    Undergraduate and postgraduate qualifications in accounting, commerce, or economics 
•    Deep knowledge of defensive asset classes including credit, debt, and cash
•    Familiarity with Australian superannuation, investment governance, and industry fund culture


Benefits that matter and make a difference for our employees


•    Leave for those moments that matter, an additional 6 days of leave at the end of year, up to 6 days paid volunteer leave, gender neutral paid parental leave of 20 weeks, Gender Affirmation leave, reproductive health and wellbeing leave, Cultural and Ceremonial leave. Access your LSL after 3 years, take AL at half pay, and purchase up to 2 weeks additional leave (just to name a few!).
•    Your Professional development matters, up to $5k per year professional development and up to 8 days professional development leave, HESTA scholarships and free access to a range of premium learning tools.
•    Your health and wellbeing matters, free annual flu shots and skin checks, incredible social events throughout the year and a comprehensive employee assistance program available 24/7.
•    Your financial wellbeing matters, up to 15% super, financial planning support, end of year payment for all Enterprise Agreement-covered employees, incentivised Employee Referral Program and novated lease options.

We celebrate, value and include people of all backgrounds, genders, identities, cultures and abilities. We welcome and support applications from First Nations people, physically, neuro or culturally diverse, LGBTQI+, and people of any age.

We are proud to be WGEA accredited as an Employer of Choice for Gender Equity. 


We want all candidates to feel safe, included and provided with the best opportunity to thrive, if you require reasonable adjustments during your application or throughout the recruitment process, please reach out to a member of the Talent team car••••@hesta.com.au and we’ll call you to discuss. 


Please note: Applications via recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this position

BI & Data Platform Analyst

Ready to pursue your professional journey with Northern Star? As an ASX 50 global-scale gold miner, we have sizeable operations in Western Australia and Alaska. With unparalleled pathways for advancement and avenues for personal growth, we stand as Australia's premier gold employer. Your journey starts here.

At Northern Star, we live by our STARR Core Values of Safety, Teamwork, Accountability, Respect and Results. These Values are not just words on paper; they are the cornerstone of our Company culture and guide everything we do. You will play a pivotal role in upholding these Values and contributing to our collective success.

Northern Star Resources currently has a unique opportunity for an experienced BI & Data Platform Analyst to join the team at our Subiaco Corporate Office, in a permanent & full-time capacity.

Reporting to the Lead - Data Analytics, this role will ensure the Company's data warehouse, data analytics, and workflow platforms are fit for purpose, reliable, secure and scalable, as well as meet current and near future business requirements. The role will work closely with data owners and consumers to ensure that data is available in the right time and place to support a data driven business. It will also include data modelling, ETL/ELT development using APIs and other source systems, integration of data from multiple systems of record, support for Power BI datasets and reports, maintenance of the existing data warehouse, and contribution to DevOps CI/CD practices.

Here is what awaits you.

Provide best practice customer service to Northern Star employees and contractors.
Plan, implement, and support existing data analytics solutions in Snowflake and other data integration solutions providing enough capacity, availability, performance, reliability, and security to support current and future enterprise reporting, analytics, and business intelligence requirements in compliance with the Information Technology Policy and other Company policies and procedures
Work collaboratively with business stakeholders to understand requirements and deliver fit-for-purpose solutions. Setup and maintain ETL pipelines and manage and optimize core data infrastructure.
Develop logical and physical data models across landing, transformation, curated, and consumption layers.
Design, develop, test, deploy, and support ETL/ELT pipelines to ingest, transform, validate, and load data from APIs, databases, flat files, and other internal and external data sources.
Develop integrations with REST APIs and other service-based interfaces to support ingestion of operational and analytical data.
Develop, maintain, and support Power BI semantic models, datasets, and reports to meet business reporting requirements as and when required.
Contribute to reporting standards, governance, usability, and data consistency across dashboards and reporting assets.
Investigate and resolve data issues, pipeline failures, reconciliation discrepancies, and performance bottlenecks.
Contribute to the implementation and ongoing improvement of DevOps and CI/CD practices for data pipelines, Snowflake deployments, and reporting deployments.
Apply data quality controls, validation checks, and monitoring across ingestion, transformation, and reporting layers.
Ensure data solutions are developed and maintained in accordance with security, privacy, governance, and retention requirements.
Provide self-service data sets for Power BI users while ensuring that sensitive and confidential information is protected from unauthorized access.
Use software development best practices, including provisions for maintaining source control, automated deployment, change management, data governance, system and data security, testing, documentation, and continuous improvement.
Maintain up to date data architecture diagrams and other supporting documentation.
Monitor the IT Service Desk and ensure that data analytics and workflow support incidents and service requests are dealt with in a timely manner, and support incidents are escalated to IT management when required.
Ensure passwords used for applications, systems, and network devices are secure and are only shared using the IT password management system.
Implement and support backup and disaster recovery solutions for data warehouse and reporting services and applications, and ensure that these services are tested at least annually
Promoting timely reporting of security incidents and aiding in the identification of and implementation of remedial actions in accordance with the Security Incident Management Plan.
Collaborate with other areas of the IT team, including service delivery, cyber, data, and business systems, to ensure alignment of technologies and operational requirements.

To thrive in this role.

Qualifications & Experience:

Degree majoring in computer studies from a recognised tertiary institution or demonstrated equivalent work experience.
Demonstrated experience in a Data Engineer, BI Engineer, or similar role within a data and analytics environment.
Significant experience working with Snowflake to develop logical and physical data models across landing, transformation, curated, and consumption layers.
Competent in developing ETL/ELT pipelines to ingest, transform, validate, and load data from APIs, databases, flat files, and other internal & external data sources using SQL & Python.
Demonstrated experience using Power BI semantic models, datasets, and reports to meet business reporting requirements.
Strong hands-on experience with Snowflake, including SQL development, data modelling, RBAC and performance tuning.
Experience designing and supporting ETL/ELT & integration pipelines, replication tasks, including ingestion from Rest APIs and other enterprise data sources.
Strong SQL, Python and data transformation skills.
Experience supporting or developing Power BI data models, datasets, and reporting solutions.
Exposure to DevOps, CI/CD, source control, and structured deployment practices.
Strong analytical, problem-solving and stakeholder engagement skills.

Passion for Growth:

Excellent communication, interpersonal, and customer service skills.
High level of integrity, professionalism, confidentiality, and attention to detail.
Strong analytical, problem-solving and stakeholder engagement skills.
Highly organised with excellent time management skills, you are resourceful & adaptive, bring a can-do attitude and can pivot & problem-solve effectively.
You are comfortable taking ownership of tasks and seeing them through to completion.

Discover the perks of working at Northern Star.

Joining the Northern Star team also offers the opportunity to enjoy our industry leading benefits and bonus programs, which include:

Career progression & professional development opportunities.
Heavily subsidised top-cover Health Insurance through GU Health.
An annual Short-term Incentive Bonus Program.
An annual Employee Share Plan Program.

Employee Assistance Programs (EAP) that includes free, confidential counselling and wellbeing services.

Eligibility.

Northern Star is an equal opportunity employer committed to achieving a diverse and inclusive workforce. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply.

Job Title: Senior Policy Analyst - AML/CTF - Financial Crime
Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
Join a Tier 1 Australian bank as a Senior Policy Analyst - Financial Crime, shaping AML/CTF frameworks at scale.

The Opportunity

This is an opportunity to step into a Senior Policy Analyst role at the heart of one of Australia's Big 4 Banks financial crime risk functions. Sitting within the Group Financial Crime team, you'll work on a global policy framework that underpins AML/CTF compliance across multiple jurisdictions, with real influence on how the organisation responds to a rapidly evolving regulatory landscape.

What You'll Be Doing

You'll own meaningful pieces of the Global Financial Crime Policy Framework, developing, reviewing and continuously improving policies, standards and guidance to keep pace with regulatory change at home and abroad. You'll monitor legislative and enforcement developments across jurisdictions, translate them into actionable updates, and work closely with senior stakeholders across risk, compliance, legal and the business to drive alignment and uplift. Expect to lead training, support assurance reviews, and contribute to the broader financial crime strategic agenda.

What They're Looking For

10+ years in financial crime across banking, consulting, legal or government
Deep knowledge of AML/CTF legislation, regulatory expectations and emerging trends
Proven experience writing, developing and managing financial crime policies, standards and programs
Exposure to international financial crime frameworks is highly regarded
Degree in Law, Business or Finance
Strong stakeholder engagement skills - you can influence at CRO and senior executive level
Commercial acumen and the ability to operate independently in a complex, matrixed environment

Join us at a defining moment

At Intelligen, we’re building something different. Over the past three years, we’ve evolved from a bold startup to a trusted partner for some of Australia’s most complex data transformations. We’ve helped organisations move from legacy to modern platforms, embedded governance into decision-making, and brought AI into the hands of the business responsibly and at speed.

As we scale into our next phase of growth, we’re looking for commercially minded, senior consulting professionals who don’t just want to sell services, but want to help shape the future of data and AI capability across Australia.

We’re looking for an experienced New Business Manager to help drive new business growth, build trusted client relationships, and create meaningful opportunities for organisations looking to unlock greater value from their data.

About the role

We are seeking an exceptional New Business Manager to drive new business growth and build trusted relationships with organisations looking to modernise, scale, and transform through data, analytics, AI, and modern cloud platforms.

This is a rare opportunity for a commercially minded consulting professional to help shape strategic client conversations across some of Australia’s most complex and data-rich organisations. You will work closely with senior customer stakeholders to understand their business priorities, identify where data can create measurable impact, and convert opportunities into high-quality consulting engagements.

You will act as a trusted advisor to executives, business leaders, technology leaders, and data leaders. You will combine strong commercial acumen with a deep understanding of data consulting to identify new opportunities, build lasting relationships, shape compelling propositions, and drive sustainable growth across target accounts and sectors.

This role is suited to someone who thrives at the intersection of strategy, sales, client advisory, and consulting. You will have autonomy to build a strong pipeline, exposure to diverse industries, and the opportunity to establish yourself as a credible voice in the Australian data consulting market.

This is for someone who can open doors, build trust, run strategic client conversations, and work with internal specialists to shape the right response to client needs.

What you’ll do
Strategic partnership and client engagement
Build trusted relationships with customer executives, business leaders, technology leaders, and data leaders.
Understand client business priorities and identify opportunities where data, analytics, AI, governance, and modern platforms can support their objectives.
Plan, prepare, and run Quarterly Business Reviews with key clients and stakeholders, using them to review progress, surface new opportunities, strengthen relationships, and align on future priorities.
Position Intelligen’s data consulting capabilities in a commercially relevant and compelling way.
Influence strategic client conversations and create opportunities for long-term partnership.
Build a deep understanding of client organisations, including their strategic priorities, operating environment, stakeholder landscape, and potential areas for growth.
New business and growth
Identify, qualify, and progress opportunities with prospective and emerging clients.
Lead the sales cycle from relationship development and opportunity qualification through to proposal development and commercial negotiation.
Identify white-space opportunities across target industries, accounts, and service areas.
Build and maintain a strong network of senior client relationships across priority markets.
Respond strategically to RFPs, proposals, and client service requests.
Maintain a robust sales pipeline and provide accurate forecasting, reporting, and updates on growth activity.
Contribute to the development of market messaging, service positioning, and go-to-market activity.
Solution positioning and pursuit leadership
Work with internal consulting, data, analytics, AI, and technical specialists to shape compelling client propositions.
Translate client business challenges into clear consulting opportunities.
Support the development of differentiated offerings, sales messaging, and market-facing materials.
Bring commercial discipline to pursuits, including qualification, value proposition development, stakeholder mapping, and deal progression.
Ensure proposals are aligned to client objectives and clearly articulate measurable business value.
Help clients understand how modern data platforms, analytics, AI, and governance can support transformation, efficiency, decision-making, and growth.
Partner with internal teams to ensure the commercial promise made to clients is clear, realistic, and aligned to Intelligen’s capability.

Team leadership and mentoring
Mentor and support junior team members involved in business development, client engagement, and consulting sales activities.
Share best practices in relationship building, consultative selling, pursuit management, and market development.
Contribute to a collaborative, high-performance culture focused on growth, quality, and client value.
Help raise the commercial capability of the broader team through coaching, knowledge sharing, and practical support.


Requirements


Minimum 7 years’ experience in data consulting, analytics consulting, technology consulting, or a closely related professional services environment (this is essential)
Must have worked in a consulting environment focused on data, including areas such as data strategy, analytics, AI, BI, cloud data platforms, data engineering, data governance, or digital transformation.
Proven experience in new business development, pipeline generation, and revenue growth, ideally within consulting OR professional services.
Demonstrated ability to identify, qualify, and convert new client opportunities from early-stage conversations through to proposal and commercial close.
Strong understanding of how consulting services are positioned, shaped, sold, and commercially negotiated.
Experience building trusted relationships with senior client stakeholders, including executives, business leaders, technology leaders, and data leaders.
Comfortable operating in ambiguity and bringing structure to complex client conversations.
Ability to lead strategic client conversations and translate business challenges into data-led consulting opportunities.
Proven experience preparing for and running QBR’s, account reviews, or strategic client planning sessions.
Strong commercial discipline, including pipeline management, opportunity qualification, forecasting, and CRM hygiene.
Confident leading discovery conversations, proposal presentations, commercial discussions, and senior stakeholder meetings.
Previous experience mentoring junior team members in business development, client engagement or client-facing roles.
Strong business acumen and ability to understand client priorities, commercial drivers, market context, and organisational challenges.
Ability to work with autonomy while collaborating effectively with internal consulting, technical, and leadership teams.
Melbourne or Sydney based, with the ability to attend client meetings and business development activity as required.


Benefits



We’re not just delivering AI and data projects, we’re humanising them. That means we care deeply about the how, not just the what. We value curiosity, creativity, and a willingness to challenge the status quo. We look for people who are driven to build a business, get curious, and offer up their opinions and ideas.

You’ll work with a team that’s smart, kind, and ambitious. You’ll have real influence in how we scale, who we hire, and how we build a high-performing culture across Sydney and ANZ. And as we grow, so will you.

As well as:

Flexibility: Work from home perks
Training & Development - every tech pathway you can think of
Free Food & Snacks (yes its a dangerous slope)
Many socials and community groups (2026 is shaping up to be really exciting…..)
Opportunity to drive projects that are of interest to you! (we love hearing about your passion projects too and what else we can be getting involved in)

Ready to help shape the team powering Australia’s next wave of responsible AI and data transformation? We’d love to meet you.

This is a high‑impact senior role for a commercially minded analytics leader who thrives on turning complex labour data into clear, actionable insight. Sitting at the intersection of finance, workforce strategy and AI‑enabled analytics, this role plays a critical part in improving productivity, managing labour cost risk and influencing organisation‑wide decision‑making.

Client Details

Our client is a major organisation within the transport sector, recognised for its commitment to safety, reliability and customer outcomes.

The organisation has a strong and genuine continuous improvement culture, encouraging innovation, data‑led decision making and intelligent use of emerging technologies such as AI. This role is part of the Finance senior leadership cohort, with regular exposure to executive and governance forums.

You'll join an organisation known for its awesome culture, collaborative leadership style and meaningful work that impacts millions of customers. Excellent work‑from‑home flexibility is supported, underpinned by trust, outcomes‑focused leadership and strong team connection.

Description

Reporting to the General Manager - Finance, the Senior Finance Labour Analytics Manager is accountable for providing strategic labour insights across the organisation using advanced analytics, financial modelling and emerging AI tools. You will lead the design and continuous improvement of sophisticated labour cost models, identify and mitigate labour cost leakage, and influence sustainable productivity improvements across a large and complex operational environment.

You will work closely with executive stakeholders and senior leaders, translating complex workforce and payroll data into compelling insights that support budgeting, forecasting, long‑range planning, investment decisions and benefits realisation.

This role is a trusted advisor to Finance, Commercial and Operations leaders and operates as a recognised subject matter expert in labour cost analytics and governance.

Provide strategic labour cost insights that materially influence financial performance and commercial outcomes
Design, govern and continuously improve advanced labour cost and productivity models, including AI‑enabled methodologies
Identify, quantify and mitigate labour cost leakage, inefficiencies and financial risk
Translate complex analytics into clear, credible insights for senior executives and governance forums

Job Title: Equity Research Analyst - Small Cap: Industrials / Metals & Mining
Ad Text:
Our client is a dynamic, high-growth institutional advisory and equities trading firm headquartered in Sydney. Committed to providing exceptional investment opportunities, where innovation meets precision and client focussed solutions.

They specialise in tailored investment strategies, capital raising, corporate advisory and trading solutions working with growth-stage companies, and institutions across Small Cap Mining & Resources or Industrials sectors. Fostering a collaborative and growth-orientated environment.

We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled individual to join our team as an Equity Research Analyst focussed on Pre IPO, Small Cap Mining & Resources or Industrials sectors. This position is responsible for delivering high-quality research across company, sector, and macroeconomic themes, providing actionable insights and strategic recommendations to Institutional Investors.

The ideal candidates will have over 6 + years industry experience as an Equities Analyst ideally with exposure to the Small or Mid Cap Industrials or Resources / Metal and Mining sectors. The roles involve equity analysis of Industrials or metals & mining stocks listed on the Australian market and the writing of research reports for investment purposes.

Additional responsibility includes regular dialogue with companies and institutional investors. You will have strong skills in analysis, report writing and an established network of institutional and industry contacts. Passion and persistence is rewarded in this swiftly evolving market.

You will be working for one of Australia's leading Institutional Equity business's, focussing on emerging companies. This is an additional hire role to add to the firms already impressive small cap research offering.
 
You'll be responsible for maintaining all stock and industry models, writing research, dealing with corporate relationships and interacting with internal and external clients. The environment is challenging and demanding, but exciting and dynamic in one of Australia's leading sell side equities and research business.

Your experience and skills

6+ years' experience in Equity Research ideally Mid/Small Caps – Industrials or Metal and Mining sectors focus.

Proven ability to critically review and analyse industry data

Highly analytical, organized and able to multitask under tight time frames

An excellent communicator, able to present recommendations and investment decisions persuasively

Competent in financial modelling, investment research, analysis and evaluation

Ideally possessing industry experience in Mid/Small Cap Mining or Industrials- but open to other sectors

This national cultural institution could be described as the memory of our nation and is responsible for preserving and enabling access to some of Australia’s most important records.

They are in the midst of a significant, multi-year transformation program focused on digitising collections, modernising legacy platforms, and repositioning data as a core strategic asset across the organisation.

The program is led by the CIO who has a track record of delivery success through building great teams.

The Opportunity

This is an opportunity to join a genuinely meaningful data platform build.

The Unified Data View project is focused on designing and delivering a centralised data capability that brings together fragmented, complex collection metadata into a single, trusted and consumable layer for analytics, reporting, and operational decision-making.

You’ll sit within a small, 7 person delivery team and play a key role in shaping how data is structured, governed, and used across the organisation.

What makes this interesting:

Greenfield-style build of an enterprise data platform sitting across deeply entrenched legacy systems
Working with highly complex, interrelated metadata where structure, lineage and meaning matter as much as the data itself
The Role 

This is a hands-on data engineering role with architectural influence. It’s not   just about building or optimising pipelines but also helping define how the data environment should work.

Design and evolve a scalable data warehouse architecture in Microsoft Fabric aligned to business rules and archival control models
Build robust ETL/ELT pipelines to ingest, transform and structure data from multiple complex source systems
Own source-to-target mapping, data modelling, and lineage to ensure transparency and trust in the data
Embed data quality, validation and governance frameworks into the platform from the ground up
The Ideal Person

This suits someone who’s comfortable working in complex, messy data environments and building structure where it doesn’t currently exist.

You’re experienced at building pipelines and also  understand data end-to-end how it’s modelled, governed, and consumed  and you’re just as comfortable in the detail as you are stepping back to understand how it needs to hold together and be used.
 

Strong experience designing and delivering data platforms in Azure/Microsoft Fabric environments
Deep capability in ETL/ELT development using Python (including PySpark/Pandas) and modern data engineering practices
Advanced Power BI capability, including data modelling, DAX, and shaping data for analytical consumption
You’ll also bring a level of maturity around documentation, lineage, and governance understanding that in this environment, clarity and traceability are critical.

Due to the nature of the project Australia Citizenship and Baseline or above is mandatory.
 
Salary / Rate

Expected rate range $140/hr - $160/hr
Labour-hire contract

Category:
Business/Systems Analysts (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
At ASIC, you can be the change that ensures a fair, strong and efficient financial system for the benefit of all Australians. Contribute to delivering on ASIC's purpose, vision, and strategic priorities to help maintain the integrity of the financial system and protect consumers from harm.

 

The Australian Government has announced the stabilisation of existing business registers and their transfer from the Australian Taxation Office (ATO) back to ASIC, under the Registry Connect (RC) Program. This program aims to deliver reliable, secure, trusted, and efficient registry services to support the economy for the benefit of all Australians.

 

Sydney or Melbourne location
24-month fixed term contract
Salary from $152,074 to $163,970 (depending on experience) + 15.4 % superannuation
Applications will close at 11:59pm AEST, Tuesday 12 May 2026

 

The team 

The Digital, Data & Technology (DD&T) Portfolio Office provides a comprehensive range of services across portfolio management, project delivery, business planning, and governance to support ASIC's strategic priorities. We work closely in partnership with ASIC Technology business units and the newly established RegistryConnect (RC) program to ensure initiatives are aligned with both strategic objectives and operational requirements.

 

The role

As a Senior Business Analysts within the RegistryConnect Program, you will employ diverse business analysis techniques, including agile methodologies, to deliver high-quality project outcomes within established timelines. Your key responsibilities will encompass eliciting, analysing and managing both high-level and detailed business requirements, conducting enterprise analysis, assessing and validating solutions and addressing both functional and non-functional requirements.

More specifically, you will: 

Collaborate with business SMEs and Technology teams to analyse and validate business needs through stakeholder engagement and workshops.
Document business, functional and non-functional requirements in clear, accessible formats using user stories for Agile projects.
Design user interfaces aligned with User Centred Design (UCD) principles and Australian Government accessibility and usability standards.
Develop process maps, including current state analysis and future-state design.
Rapidly understand business structures and strategies to align work with organisational goals.
Support test planning and execution activities.
Assist with UAT preparation, system implementation and development of user guides and training materials.
Demonstrate strong self management and accountability in delivering team and organisational priorities.

 

About you

The ideal candidate this role will be an experienced, proactive and detail-oriented Business Analyst professional who possess strong analytical and communication skills. The ideal candidate will excel at translating complex business needs into clear, actionable requirements and thrive working within a collaborative environment with both business and IT stakeholders. They should also have a strong background in business analysis techniques, including Agile methodologies, Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) and be adept at delivering high-quality project outcomes within established timelines.

In addition, you will have:

Relevant tertiary qualifications or professional certifications, with Agile and SAFe certification being highly desirable.
Excellent knowledge of SDLC and Agile methodology.
Strong skills in eliciting, documenting, and managing requirements.
Experienced with industry process modelling and requirements management tools.
Demonstrated technology-based project experience within in large complex enterprise environments.
Exceptional analytical and enterprise-level problem-solving skills in IT systems and complex issue resolution.
Proven ability to independently manage multiple tasks or projects with full accountability for outcomes.
Skilled in delivering professional verbal and written communication to diverse audiences, with a strong commitment to customer service excellence.
Demonstrable experience in integrating different business applications and improving efficiency of workflows and processes.
Strong knowledge and previous experience working with systems and technology such as SAS, Microsoft Dynamics, Java, Siebel/Oracle fusion, Peoplesoft and Data Warehouse, highly desirable.
An existing Australian Government security clearance (Baseline/NV1) is highly desirable, however candidates without a current clearance will also be considered.

 

Click ‘apply' to start your application. Applications will close at 11:59pm AEST, Tuesday 12 May 2026.

ASIC's remit is one of the broadest of regulators across the world. ASIC regulates corporations, markets, financial services and consumer credit and monitors and promotes market integrity and consumer protection in the Australian financial system. Through our enforcement work, we hold to account those who contravene the law, working to achieve strong outcomes that address the greatest consumer and investor harms. 

ASIC is an equal opportunity employer seeking people who want to make a difference. ASIC is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace where the very best talent in Australia chooses to work. Flexible work arrangements
Assistance for study and professional development

ACCIONA is a global company, leading in the development of regenerative infrastructure that creates a positive impact on society. Our workforce consists of more than 41,500 professionals, present in more than 40 countries across the five continents, all contributing in our mission to design a better planet. Are you a passionate individual who wants to make a difference, promote sustainable development, and find solutions to the biggest global challenges, including climate change, overpopulation, and water scarcity? Come and join us in building the infrastructure our planet needs to achieve a sustainable future.

The Opportunity

This role plays a key part in driving continuous improvement, strengthening internal controls, and supporting change management initiatives across the ICT function. You will be responsible for identifying and remediating control gaps, enhancing and redesigning processes, contributing to the annual audit program, and supporting effective stakeholder engagement throughout change activities.

A key focus of the role is to track, prioritise, and manage improvement opportunities to ensure ongoing operational uplift. Reporting to the ICT Performance and Improvement Manager, you will help champion operational excellence and support the delivery of a high‑performing, well‑governed ICT function.

Key responsibilities:


Lead the assessment, documentation, and remediation of internal control gaps, maintaining a robust ICT controls framework and reporting progress using data‑driven insights.
Drive process improvement initiatives by analysing data, benchmarking performance, and prioritising optimisation opportunities across the ICT function.
Manage the internal audit program end‑to‑end, including annual planning, execution, documentation of findings, follow‑up actions, and coordination with external auditors.
Redesign ICT processes to improve quality and efficiency, ensuring high‑quality process documentation (process maps, RACIs, SOPs) and alignment with industry best practice and ACCIONA standards.
Support effective change management and stakeholder engagement by assessing change impacts, developing high‑level change plans

About You:

Strong background in compliance, audit, and process improvement (ideally with professional services/Big 4 experience), with demonstrated success leading change and engaging stakeholders.
Excellent communication and leadership skills, with proven ability to design, document, and embed new or redesigned processes.
Deep experience with internal control frameworks, gap assessment methodologies, and continuous improvement practices.
Bachelor's degree in IT, Business, or related field, with preferred certifications in ITIL, Agile, Change Management, or Internal Audit, and 8+ years' experience in ICT operations or performance improvement.

Support campaigns, feature launches and investor initiatives

AEMO at the Heart of Energy   

AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians.  Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this. We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection. Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector.    

About the role 

The Operations division is responsible for operating today’s systems and market securely and reliably, along with supporting AEMO to navigate the energy future. Within the Market Management department of the Operations division, sits the WA Prudentials and Settlements team which is responsible for the sound financial management of the Western Australian Wholesale Electricity Market (WEM). The functions include settlement, prudential management, metering operations. The role level is flexible based upon the applicant’s experience. 

Working autonomously, you will use your well-developed analytical skills and subject matter expertise to identify, analyse, define, test and implement rule, process and system changes that improve the efficiency and effectiveness of operational processes and systems. Working towards becoming a subject matter expert, you will contribute to strategic projects related to market enhancement and process improvement projects through the provision of analysis, option development and recommendations on solutions. 

This role can be based in Perth or Sydney. 

What you will be doing  

Working independently or in small groups within the team to perform metering and settlement functions in compliance with the ESM Rules and WEM Procedures within defined deadlines.

Identify and undertake root cause analysis, reporting, risk assessment and implementation of fixes or mitigations related to technical or compliance issues.

Develop and maintain open, supportive working relationships with team members and other teams, establish effective networks across the business and contribute to agreed team outcomes.

Using an understanding of systems, rules and processes, undertake complex quantitative and qualitative analysis which assess market outcomes to inform decision making and support projects.

Recommend process and procedure improvement activities and projects, that reduce compliance risk, improve operational efficiency and deliver improved market outcomes. 

What you will bring to the role  

Degree in economics, computing, commerce, engineering, science or a related discipline.

Experience in a commercial or regulatory environment. Including interpreting and operating in compliance with technical documents and/or legal instruments, such as rules and regulations.

Proven qualitative and quantitative analytical skills that provide technically resourceful, evidenced based solutions to issues and execute solutions in a constructive and timely manner.

Ability to establish and maintain strong collaborative relationships across a range of internal and external stakeholders, including market participants and AEMO staff.

Demonstrated ability to develop high quality written reports and deliver effective presentations which communicate technical, complex concepts or data analysis to informed audiences.

In return some of our benefits to you 

Flexible working: work from home, part time, job share, hybrid options, and additional leave options 

Professional development via projects, industry networks, job rotation, study assistance and more. 

Give back with up to 4 days of volunteering leave per year.  

Reward your hard work with annual performance bonuses (subject to eligibility). 

Embrace a healthier you with our wellness program, discounted health insurance, gym perks and our comprehensive Employee Assistance Program (EAP).  

About Our Process  

AEMO values diversity and inclusivity in the workplace, welcoming applications from all backgrounds without regard to age, disability, gender, sexual orientation, parental status, race, religion and our First Nation peoples. We are dedicated to accommodating applicants' needs during the application or interview process, simply let us know by emailing tal•••@aemo.com.au.

Analytics Lead, Procurement | Foodbuy
Sydney office based, Mitchell Street, McMahons Point, NSW, 2060 
Perth office is the next preferred location
Reporting to the National Commercial Manager
Hybrid working model (2 days working from home & 3 days working in the office)

 

Compass Group Australia generates $2 billion in annual revenue, serving 350+ clients across 700+ sites with a team of 16,000. Through Foodbuy, our dedicated procurement and supply chain organization, we offer unmatched scale and expertise in sourcing, contracting, and supply management.

Join a global leader that champions innovation, empowers careers, and creates possibilities! Foodbuy is seeking a Senior Procurement Analyst to provide high-quality commercial insight and analytical support to our procurement and sourcing teams.

 
About the Role

This role is critical in supporting sourcing events, supplier negotiations, and contracting activities through robust data analysis, commercial modelling, and performance tracking. You will partner closely with senior procurement and finance stakeholders, acting as a trusted commercial advisor and helping drive value across Foodbuy’s procurement portfolios.

This is a team lead and hands-on analytical role with strong exposure to senior decision-makers, ideal for someone who enjoys working with complex data and influencing outcomes through insight rather than people management.

Key Responsibilities

Commercial & Procurement Analytics

Provide commercial and analytical support across Foodbuy procurement portfolios.
Deliver value-based insights to inform sourcing, negotiation, and contracting decisions.
Perform net pricing, scenario, leakage, and inflation analysis.
Develop financial and pricing models to support sourcing events and deal structuring.
Provide commercial insight and challenge during contract reviews.
Track deal and contract performance against forecast outcomes.
Analyse and report on procurement initiatives, pipeline activity, and usage trends.

Commercial Modelling & Data Capability

Develop and maintain commercial models and templates tailored to procurement categories and events.
Support supplier tenders by assisting with commercial models and value criteria.
Maintain product cost mapping, including detailed supply-chain value breakdowns.
Translate complex datasets into clear, actionable insights for stakeholders.
Use tools such as Excel, Tableau, SQL, and Microsoft Access to support analysis and reporting.

Stakeholder Engagement & Ways of Working

Act as a trusted commercial partner to Procurement, Finance, and broader business stakeholders.
Communicate insights clearly and professionally to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Build strong working relationships within the Foodbuy commercial and procurement teams.
Contribute to a collaborative, service-focused team culture.
Participate in commercial projects aimed at improving business performance and reducing costs.

Team Leadership

Provide day-to-day guidance, coaching, and technical support to procurement analysts
Review analytical outputs to ensure accuracy, consistency, and commercial relevance
Contribute to the development of best-practice commercial models, templates, and standards
Act as a role model for strong commercial thinking, collaboration, and stakeholder engagement

 

Skills & Experience
Essential

Tertiary qualification in STEM, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline.
Strong understanding of product pricing, costing, and commercial principles.
Advanced Excel capability (including lookups, pivot tables, and financial modelling).
Experience working with large and complex datasets.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to generate commercial insights.
Sound business acumen with clear, professional communication skills.
Ability to manage competing priorities and meet deadlines.

Desirable

Experience in procurement, sourcing, negotiation, or contracting environments.
Exposure to Tableau, SQL, and/or Microsoft Access.
Experience within food, catering, FMCG, or a related industry.
Degree in Supply Chain or relevant CIPS qualification.
5 years’ post-graduate experience in an analytical, commercial, or procurement-focused role.

 

Ready to make your mark? Apply now and be part of something bigger. If you enjoy turning data into commercial insight and influencing procurement outcomes, this is an opportunity to play a key role in delivering value across Foodbuy’s sourcing and contracting portfolio.

 

Why Compass Group

Be part of Australia’s largest food and support services company. You’ll work across iconic venues and leading workplaces, partnering with experts in culinary, technology and sustainability to deliver experiences people love. We offer scale, career pathways, and the autonomy to shape strategies that deliver meaningful, measurable growth.

 

The Benefits

Working with a large global company means you can be assured that we have an attractive benefits program! This includes comprehensive learning & development offerings, a variety of recognition programs and company discounts, an excellent salary benchmarking matrix and at this management level also includes an incentive bonus scheme! You can read all about these here: Why work at Compass

If you're ready to take the next step in your marketing career and contribute to something bigger, we want to hear from you! For any questions, doubts, or for a confidential chat, please reach out to us at careers@compass-group.com.au

This sector-leading, innovative, purpose led, not-for-profit organisation is dedicated to providing lasting social impact by putting people at the centre of what they do.  They operate with a strong commercial focus, ensuring that safe, secure and affordable housing is provided across Victoria.


The Position
The Risk and Performance Manager, as part of the Senior Leadership team, is responsible for maintaining and strengthening risk management, business continuity, planning, and performance frameworks. This opportunity leads the facilitation of risk workshops, oversees updates to Business Impact Analyses and Business Continuity Plans, and coordinates the organisational annual business planning cycle and performance reporting. 


The Person
To be successful in this role, you will have at least five years experience across:

Our client is a large Western Australian organisation operating at the forefront of data-driven intelligence. The nature of their work means they cannot be named at this stage — but we can tell you the problems are complex, the stakes are high, and the technology is cutting-edge.

The Role
You'll join a multi-vendor project team as a Graph Data Developer, contributing to an in-flight graph analytics platform built on Neo4j and GraphAware Hume. This is a hands-on role — you'll be designing and building graph database solutions, integrating data pipelines, and working closely with stakeholders to turn complex requirements into high-quality analytical capabilities.
If you're someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment and wants to see your work make a genuine difference, this is the role for you.

Responsibilities

Design, develop, and implement graph data solutions to meet analytical and operational needs
Build and optimise Neo4j graph architectures, including schema and data modelling
Develop and integrate graph analytics capabilities within an existing enterprise platform
Contribute to technical design discussions and help drive continuous improvement
Deliver work on time and to a high standard — including testing, documentation, and deployment
Engage with technical and non-technical stakeholders to ensure solutions hit the mark
Uphold strict information security and data confidentiality standards
Key Experience

Hands-on experience with Neo4j and Cypher query language
Strong Python skills for data ingestion, transformation, and pipeline orchestration
Experience developing and maintaining data pipelines and SQL transformations
Practical problem-solver — you find pragmatic solutions that deliver real value
Comfortable hitting the ground running in a fast-moving, in-flight project
Desirable Experience

Experience with graph data science, ML, NLP, or LLM-driven analytics components
Familiarity with enterprise or cloud-based analytics environments
Exposure to multi-vendor project delivery
Azure DevOps or Kanban board experience
Public sector background — intelligence domain a bonus

The Benefits

Work on one of WA's most sophisticated intelligence analytics platforms
Join a collaborative, multi-disciplinary team using leading graph technologies
Contribute to outcomes that genuinely matter to the community

The Opportunity

We’re looking for an experienced Data Scientist who can own projects end-to-end, deliver sharp insights, and continuously improve machine learning and analytics products. You’ll work closely with stakeholders, solve complex problems, and lead new data initiatives from idea to execution.

Turn complex data into clear, actionable insights through delivering fast, accurate analysis and bringing it to life with using visualisation such as Tableau or Databricks dashboards.

Principal Value Consultant, ANZ

High Impact, Influential Individual Contributor (initially) Role.
Significant Pre and Post Sale Involvement.
Supportive Culture with Training, Hybrid Work Flexibility.
Based in Sydney, International (APAC) & Interstate Travel.
Attractive Salary + Commission + LTI.

About The Company

Robert half Executive Search has been retained to identify a Principal Value Consultant to join a well-established and market leading global B2B Enterprise SaaS technology vendor offering mission-critical solutions to Insurers.

The firm boasts an unparalleled implementation track record of successful projects, supported by the largest R&D team and partner ecosystem in the industry. With the industry going through a technology-led transformation and a significant shift in subscription-led applications, the company has experienced continuous YoY annual revenue growth and is well-positioned for further significant growth over the next 3-5 years.

This is a newly created senior, market-facing pre-sales role responsible for shaping early-stage customer engagement and driving demand for the firm's solutions across ANZ. The Principal Value Consultant will play a critical role in opening new markets, initiating strategic conversations with prospective clients, and elevating the firm's value proposition at the earliest stages of the sales cycle.

Value Consulting is a niche, high-impact department within this firm and the successful individual will be the 'economic architect' of the sales cycle, serving as the 'Value North Star' for ANZ Sales, Marketing, and Professional Services.

More about the role:

This role will be pivotal in strengthening the firm's value selling capability, particularly in building customer-driven business cases and enhancing discovery excellence across the regional team. A core mandate of the role is to move beyond traditional solution-led selling-partnering with customers to deeply understand their priorities and co-develop a compelling case for change and investment.

Key responsibilities include supporting demand creation, improving the quality and depth of customer discovery, and enabling clients to articulate the financial and strategic rationale for transformation. While the primary focus is on net-new customer acquisition, the role will also support strategic discussions within existing accounts.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead 'Value Engagements': brief, intense, multi-day workshops that include direct operational observation, benefit estimation, and presenting the 'Business Case for Change' to C-suite decision-makers.
Lead, support and advise on 'Business Optimisation Engagements': to functionally assess the utilisation of the installed platform to provide recommendations of how to address observed challenges and pain points.
Strategic Advisory: Lead customer discussions on their transformation agenda and how they can maximise their business value through the platform.
Value Discovery: Conduct deep-dive workshops to uncover process inefficiencies in business value chains, translating these into quantifiable ROI and TCO models.
Executive Storytelling: Present financial and technical findings in a compelling manner to CFOs, CTOs, and 'Heads of'.
Market Influence: Promote the firm's value methodology at regional industry events and public conferences.

Required Attributes:

A background in Value-Led Sales, or Pre-Sales for Core System Enterprise SaaS vendors, ideally in InsurTech though other broader technologies would also be of interest if serving an Insurance or BFSI industry vertical.
Ideally some exposure to Management Consulting (Big 4/Tier 1), and /or Systems Integrators.
General Insurance fluency (claims, policies, underwriting, billing).
Business Value Engineering: TCO Analysis, Value realisation / Value leakage, Sensitivity analysis, ROI calculations with a strong ability to read Balance Sheets and financial statements.
Process Optimisation: Business Process Re-engineering, Process standardisation, straight-through processing - Prior experience in Process Improvement methodologies such as Six Sigma and others.
Workshop Mastery: Proven track record of facilitating design-thinking or discovery workshops with senior stakeholders.
Cloud Strategy: Experience developing business cases specifically for SaaS/Cloud migrations, highlighting the shift from CapEx to OpEx.
Exceptional active listening skills, able to generate consensus among conflicting stakeholders, and pivot strategy on your feet.
High aptitude for mental arithmetic and the ability to build robust cost-benefit analyses for multi-million-dollar enterprise projects.
Executive Discovery, Storytelling and Executive Stakeholder Management.

Senior Business Analyst
 
Role Purpose
 
We are seeking a versatile and highly skilled Senior Business Analyst to join our team. This role is designed for a "Core BA" who enjoys the full spectrum of analysis—from the early stages of discovery and strategic planning to the final stages of delivery and business readiness.
 
We value a holistic approach to analysis. While delivery is a key component, this role requires a professional who is equally passionate about the foundational work: identifying business rules, mapping complex processes, and ensuring the organization is fully prepared for a smooth transition into Business-as-Usual (BAU).
 
Key Responsibilities

Comprehensive Project Lifecycle: Lead the end-to-end analytical journey, including initial feasibility studies ("does the idea fly?"), detailed requirements gathering, and post-delivery change support.
Process & Business Logic: Deep-dive into existing data and document "As-Is" and "To-Be" states with a focus on creating clear, robust business rules and process maps.
Operational Readiness: Partner with the business to define what is required for successful adoption, ensuring all communication and training needs are identified and addressed.
Independent Stream Leadership: Take ownership of specific project workstreams—ranging from large-scale systems implementations to digital customer experience enhancements—acting as a proactive lead for your area.
Strategic Growth & Innovation: Assist in defining the requirements for new products and service tiers, ensuring they align with broader strategic goals.
Skills & Experience

Balanced Methodology: You are comfortable working across various delivery frameworks. You excel in environments that require thoughtful documentation and upfront planning alongside active delivery.
The "Analyst's Toolkit": Expert proficiency in process modeling, data interrogation, and translating complex business needs into actionable functional requirements.
Collaborative Engagement: A natural ability to build rapport with diverse stakeholders, from technical digital teams to operational site managers.
Self-Directed & Proactive: A proven track record of operating independently, taking initiative, and driving outcomes without the need for constant oversight.
Personal Attributes

Analytical Depth: You look beyond the surface to understand the "why" and ensure the "how" is sustainable.
Energetic & Positive: You bring a "can-do" attitude and a commitment to fostering a supportive, high-performing team culture.
Self-Starter: You are naturally curious and take the lead in identifying and solving problems within your scope.

Key responsibilities

Job Title: Senior Manager – Atmos Solutions + Digital Products
Ad Text:
Atmos: Where Purpose, Growth and Humanity Shape Exceptional Careers

Senior Manager – Atmos Solutions + Digital Products

 

Why Atmos 

Atmos exists to revolutionise cyber, privacy and digital risk management, building the next generation of innovative legal services for a world where technology, crisis and law are permanently connected.

 

We are building something distinctive in the market, shaping the future of cyber, privacy and digital risk. We are constantly innovating and evolving, improving how we operate and streamlining the work that slows others down, giving you more time to focus on meaningful client impact, your development and your wellbeing.

 

Here, you grow faster and contribute meaningfully, working alongside senior leaders who are invested in your development and share how they think, decide and lead.

 

We empower you to do the best work of your career, fostering curiosity, continuous learning and genuine flexibility that supports both professional performance and a life outside work.

Your Mission

Lead, build and scale Atmos’ Solutions + Digital Products ecosystem, owning the end‑to‑end lifecycle from product strategy and design through delivery, optimisation and next‑generation innovation.

 

In this role, you’ll lead a high‑performing team of legal professionals and deliver technology‑enabled services that transform how cyber incidents, disputes, investigations and litigation are managed, operating with autonomy, commercial judgement and real impact from day one.

You will:

Lead and scale the Atmos Solutions + Digital Products team (10+ professionals), including hiring, capability uplift, performance and financial management.
Own the delivery of Atmos’s proprietary solutions, including Alert NOW, Review NOW, Notify NOW, Protect NOW and Trace NOW.
Drive end‑to‑end delivery of legal tech, eDiscovery and AI‑enabled solutions across disputes, investigations and crisis response matters.
Partner closely with Atmos leadership, lawyers and clients to align delivery with business priorities and client outcomes.
Identify workflow bottlenecks and deliver productivity, quality and growth initiatives across professional services delivery.
Scout, trial and operationalise emerging AI /GenAI, automation and legal technology, embedding best practice into scalable workflows.
Lead the vendor and ALSP ecosystem, working across procurement, technology and operations to select and manage fit‑for‑purpose solutions.
Act as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders on complex, high‑pressure, often multi‑jurisdictional matters.
Build reusable assets - playbooks, templates, training and enablement programs to scale capability across the firm.

 This is legal technology at the frontline of crisis, regulation and litigation.

You Might Be

A senior legal technology or eDiscovery professional who:

Has 6–8+ years’ experience delivering complex litigation or investigation matters, ideally in Big Six, Big Four or leading vendors.
Brings deep hands‑on expertise across modern eDiscovery platforms, analytics and AI/GenAI tooling.
Is comfortable operating strategically and rolling up your sleeves when required.
Enjoys leading and developing high‑performing teams with a human‑first mindset.
Can translate complex technical detail into clear, defensible advice for senior stakeholders.
Is curious, commercially minded and energised by fast‑moving, high‑impact environments.

 

Experience in cyber, disputes, investigations or regulated professional services is strongly advantageous but mindset, judgement and leadership matter most.

Why Atmos

Atmos is building something distinctive in the market, shaping the future of cyber, privacy and digital risk. We are constantly innovating and evolving, improving how we operate and streamlining the work that slows others down, giving you more time to focus on meaningful client impact, your development and your wellbeing.

 

Here, you grow faster and contribute meaningfully, working alongside senior leaders who are invested in your development and share how they think, decide and lead.

We empower you to do the best work of your career, fostering curiosity, continuous learning and genuine flexibility that support both professional performance and a life outside work.

We offer:

A market-leading bonus structure (including innovation rewards)
Merit-based progression
Hybrid work and genuine flexibility
AI-enabled systems and operational support

 

Our culture is built on four guiding principles:

We are humans first, looking out for each other and our clients.

We deliver solutions and are willing to do things differently.

We move at pace, keep things simple and deliver on our promises.

We value our people and recognise their contribution.

 

The Bigger Vision

Atmos exists to revolutionise cyber, privacy and digital risk management, building the next generation of innovative legal services for a world where technology, crisis and law are permanently connected.

If you want to help shape that future, we should talk.

face2face reference: 670385

Kompozition is a forward-thinking Australian company helping organisations design and deliver complex systems more efficiently. We use a unique, leading-edge digital engineering platform based on the behaviour modelling language (BML) to help our clients understand and solve big, complex problems.

About the Role

The Senior Engineer will play a key role in supporting clients to model complex systems using the Kompozition Platform. You will help clients understand and improve their operations, build high-quality models, and translate complex information into actionable engineering artefacts.

This role is ideal for a technically minded professional with 10+ years of engineering experience who enjoys working with customers, facilitating workshops, and applying systems thinking in fast-paced environments. Defence or Federal Government experience is highly desirable.

What We're Looking For

We’re looking for someone who enjoys solving problems, working with people, and learning new tools and ideas. If you enjoy helping others succeed, can quickly pick up technical concepts, and want to make a real impact, this could be the role for you.

We welcome people from all walks of life—including those new to the tech, government or Defence industry. Our ideal team member is:

An individual who has strong observation, analytical and problem-solving skills
A strong communicator who enjoys working with others
Organised, reliable, and able to manage their time well
Comfortable learning new tools and explaining them to others
Curious and always looking to improve
Happy to work onsite with clients (mostly in Canberra)

Familiarity with systems thinking, modelling tools such as Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) tools, or process mapping
An interest in technology, design, or systems engineering
Previous Defence or Government experience
Experience in customer support, consulting, or analysis roles

What You'll Do \ Key Responsibilities

Client Engagement & Support

Onboard, train, and enable client teams in the Kompozition platform.
Facilitate workshops to help clients capture intent, define objectives, and structure information.
Support clients in developing, validating, and maintaining system models.
Collaborate with Customer Success and Kompozition’s internal teams to ensure client adoption, satisfaction, and retention.
Act as the client voice internally by sharing insights with Kompozition’s internal teams.
Track and report client progress against agreed outcomes.

Digital Engineering & Modelling

Create digital system models and generate artefacts such as needs, requirements, tests, and workflows.
Apply systems thinking to understand complex interfaces, data flows, and operational scenarios.
Analyse technical documentation to derive requirements and identify key system behaviours.
Establish and maintain traceability from business goals to engineering artefacts.

Consulting & Technical Leadership

Represent requirements using diagrams, scenarios, or prototypes where appropriate.
Conduct impact analysis, manage requirement changes, and maintain documentation quality.
Support client engineering, testing, and development teams by clarifying requirements and ensuring alignment.

Essential Requirements

Australian Citizenship (due to government & Defence-related work)
The successful candidate must hold, or be eligible to obtain, an Australian Government Security Vetting Agency (AGSVA) security clearance
Qualifications and/or experience in a STEM-related field (e.g. Engineering, IT/ Programming, Technical Analysis)

Company description:



Insignia Financial Limited is an Australian financial services company which offers a range of products and services including financial advice, superannuation, investment management and trustee services



Job description:



Senior Risk and Governance Specialist

Make risk real and help us make better decisions
Put your fingerprints on the future to progress your career
Embed risk that enables safe and trusted acceleration of Technology

The opportunity to join our team

We're looking for a Senior Risk and Governance Specialist to join our 1st Line Technology Risk team and play a key role in shaping how technology and cloud risk is managed across Insignia Financial. In this role you'll partner closely with a key business unit as its applications transition into and operate within cloud environments, helping to identify, assess and manage technology risks while helping teams make confident, well-informed decisions.

You'll work closely with teams who are actively building and operating in the cloud, gaining exposure to multiple cloud technologies and cross-functional teams.

The cloud environment is still maturing, which means your work will have real impact. You'll help shape how technology risk is managed in a cloud-first organisation, guiding teams through new challenges and strengthening controls as the environment evolves. On top of that, you'll be part of a close, collaborative team that values support, open communication, and a bit of fun along the way.

This is a hands-on, first line technology risk role giving you the opportunity to influence how risk is managed as the organisation continues its cloud-first journey.

Key Responsibilities:

Act as the first-line Technology Risk partner to a dedicated technology business unit
Support risk identification, assessment and remediation across cloud-hosted applications
Provide pragmatic guidance on technology and cloud risk matters
Work closely with cloud platform teams
Manage and track technology incidents, issues and remediation actions
Contribute to technology risk uplift initiatives and governance improvements

What you will bring

You're someone who understands technology risk but is equally comfortable engaging with technical teams. You enjoy working closely with stakeholders, asking the right questions, and helping teams think through risk in a practical way rather than simply applying frameworks.

Strong experience in first-line Technology Risk
Exposure to cloud environments, ideally AWS (Azure or GCP also valuable)
Ability to engage confidently with technical teams and stakeholders
Understanding of technology risk frameworks and governance practices
Knowledge of the Australian regulatory landscape (APRA CPS 234, CPS 230 etc.)
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills

Find the better way

What gets us excited about working here? Helping people feel good about their money as one of Australia's leading financial wellbeing organisations. From those starting out to those retiring. As your team, we'll show up for you and together we will create financial wellbeing for every Australian. Because we know people who are confident about their financial future live better lives.

If this sounds like your kind of career, you sound like our kind of person. With us, you'll do work that builds your technical know-how and challenges our entire industry to move forward. Along the way, you'll be free to explore new ideas and technology, solve problems in a team, and independently to get great things done. Around here, we like to call it "putting our fingerprints on the future".

People-inspired tech

Deep expertise combined with creativity can take on the trickiest of problems. Join our team of brilliant minds and mentors as we explore our way to innovation and apply technology in more human ways than ever.

To Apply

Read more here about why you should join our team.

Applicants will be required to provide evidence of their eligibility to work in Australia, and at a minimum be required to undertake police and basic credit checks as a condition of employment.

Please note that applications from agencies will not be considered at this time.

We acknowledge and celebrate the richness that individual differences bring to our team. If you need assistance or an adjustment during the application process, please reach out and let us know.

Temple & Webster is Australia’s #1 online destination for the home. We’re a tech-led retailer, a logistics powerhouse, and a team of creative problem-solvers.  We don’t just move boxes; we build the infrastructure that allows millions of Australians to live more beautifully.  We are ambitious, we are fast-paced, and we are obsessed with delivering a seamless customer experience.

The Role
We aren't looking for a "maintainer" - we’re looking for an architect of scale.  As the Head of Supply Chain Technology, you will own the technical engine that drives our most complex operations.  You will sit at the intersection of physical logistics and digital engineering, transforming our ERP from a system of record into a strategic competitive advantage.


This is your chance to lead high-stakes digital transformation in a business where the supply chain is the very heart of the customer journey.
Why this role will excite you
True ERP Solution Ownership: You define the architecture and roadmap for our ERP to ensure it supports a scaling retail giant.
Bridge the "One Tech" Divide: No more siloed departments. You’ll be the primary liaison between Supply Chain and the broader Engineering teams, synchronizing delivery methodologies and representing the function on the Change Advisory Board (CAB).
High-Stakes Innovation: From 3PL visibility and SaaS integration to AI-driven planning, you’ll have the mandate to explore emerging tech that solves real-world bottlenecks.
Commercial Empowerment: You’ll own the SCT budget and P&L, giving you the autonomy to drive cost-governance and reinvest savings into high-impact innovation.
Leadership that Matters: You’ll mentor a specialised team, fostering a culture of technical excellence, continuous improvement, and professional growth.
Who You Are
The "Translator": You speak fluent "Logistics" and "Engineering." You can take a complex operational pain point and turn it into a clean, scalable technical specification.
ERP Veteran: You’ve managed and scaled Enterprise platforms within a high-volume e-commerce, retail, or distribution environment.
Systems Thinker: You look at the supply chain end-to-end - from supplier origin to the customer’s doorstep - and you see exactly where the data needs to flow better.
Strategically Grounded: You enjoy high-level strategic planning but are equally comfortable diving into the details of ISO-aligned risk frameworks or vendor negotiations to get the best outcome.
Why T&W?
The Environment: A stunning HQ in St Peters with a hybrid rhythm with 2 days WFH.
The Culture: We take our work seriously, but not ourselves. Enjoy daily mindfulness, catered team lunches, and a collaborative energy that encourages fresh ideas.
The Rewards: Competitive salary, paid birthday leave, generous parental leave, and a significant staff discount to help you live more beautifully.
The Impact: You’ll be a key player in an ASX-listed company that still moves with the agility and spirit of a startup.
Our supply chain is the backbone of our success. Are you ready to build the tech that powers it?

Reporting to the CEO, this new role will lead sector engagement, policy development and advocacy to shape and advance Australia’s homelessness policy agenda.

Leadership & Influence – Ability to lead complex processes, inspire collaboration and influence decision-makers to shift policy, systems and public understanding.
The Benefits

This is a rare opportunity to have an impact and strengthen homelessness systems to reduce the incidence and impact of homelessness. You will leave a legacy and build trusted relationships across complex stakeholder environments, while maintaining a focus on delivering outputs that are practical, accessible and valued by the sector.
